skill.name: agentix-ceo
skill.description: Manage your team — create roles, assign tasks, spawn workers, and monitor progress
skill.summary: Orchestrate AI worker teams via the Agentix platform API for autonomous task execution.
skill.author: agentix-cloud

security_audit.summary: Evaluated a 266-line SKILL.md documentation file describing the Agentix CEO API. The static scanner flagged 129 potential issues, but on review all are false positives. The file is a markdown API reference — it contains bash code blocks (heredocs, mkdir, export) as documentation examples, URL references for API endpoints, and environment variable names — none of which represent executable code. The 'CRITICAL' and 'HIGH' heuristic findings (code execution + network + credentials) are expected for any API integration skill that reads/writes local credentials and makes HTTPS calls to a documented API. No prompt injection, obfuscation, or malicious intent detected. Risk level: low due to handling of API keys and GitHub tokens, but behavior is legitimate and well-documented.

low_findings.title: Credential storage in plaintext file
low_findings.description: The skill reads and writes API keys, GitHub tokens, and Anthropic API keys to ~/.agentix/credentials in plaintext format. While this is a standard pattern, the file lacks explicit file permission hardening (e.g., chmod 600).

low_findings.title: Sensitive token handling in API calls
low_findings.description: The skill instructs users to provide GitHub tokens and Anthropic API keys that are then transmitted to the Agentix API. Tokens are never displayed in chat per the skill's own security guidance, but transmission to a third-party API carries inherent risk.

content.user_title: Orchestrate AI worker teams through Agentix
content.value_statement: Managing multiple AI workers across tasks is complex and error-prone. This skill provides a structured API reference for creating roles, assigning tasks, spawning workers, and monitoring progress on the Agentix platform.
actual_capabilities: Register and authenticate with the Agentix SaaS platform or self-hosted instance
Create, read, update, and delete team roles with custom system prompts
Create and manage tasks across statuses: backlog, ready, in_progress, review, done
Spawn and resume AI workers to execute tasks via API calls
Monitor team activity through an event feed with task-specific filtering
limitations: Requires an Agentix platform account (SaaS) or self-hosted instance to function
Workers need a separately provided Anthropic API key to execute tasks
Self-hosted mode is unauthenticated and intended for local network use only
No built-in code execution — this skill is an API reference, not an autonomous agent runner

use_cases.title: Engineering team lead
use_cases.description: Coordinate multiple AI coding agents across a software project, assigning specialized roles like backend-engineer and code-reviewer to different tasks.

use_cases.title: DevOps automation architect
use_cases.description: Set up self-hosted Agentix instances for internal team automation with CI/CD integration via GitHub tokens.

use_cases.title: AI workflow researcher
use_cases.description: Experiment with multi-agent orchestration patterns using supervised and autopilot modes for academic or prototype work.

prompt_templates.title: Initial setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up the Agentix CEO skill. Check if I have existing credentials, and if not, guide me through the registration flow.

prompt_templates.title: Create a development team
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new team called 'web-app-dev' with a goal to build a React dashboard. Add roles for frontend-engineer, backend-engineer, and code-reviewer.

prompt_templates.title: Spawn a worker for a task
prompt_templates.prompt: List all tasks in 'ready' status for my team, then spawn a worker for the highest-priority one.

prompt_templates.title: Monitor team activity
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the last 20 events for my team and summarize which workers are currently running and what they're doing.

output_examples.input: Create a new team called 'docs-team' with a goal to maintain technical documentation
output: Creating team 'docs-team' with goal: maintain technical documentation
Team created successfully. Team ID: cmm3x7k9p2
You can now create roles and tasks for this team.
Checking worker status for team cmm3x7k9p2...
- worker_abc123: Task 'Update API docs' — in progress (4m 32s)
- worker_def456: Task 'Fix broken links' — in progress (1m 15s)

best_practices: Always read the playbook at the start of every session to apply team-specific rules and mode settings
Reuse existing teams and roles across sessions rather than creating duplicates
Never display API keys, tokens, or secrets in chat output — read and write them silently to the credentials file
anti_patterns: Creating a new team every session instead of reusing the existing team ID
Displaying API keys or tokens in chat output for debugging purposes
Spawning workers without user approval when operating in supervised mode

faq.question: What is the difference between SaaS and self-hosted modes?
faq.answer: SaaS uses https://agentix.cloud with registration and API key authentication. Self-hosted points to your own instance URL, requires no API key, and is intended for local network use.

faq.question: Where are credentials stored?
faq.answer: Credentials are stored in plaintext in ~/.agentix/credentials. The file contains API keys, team ID, and customer ID for SaaS mode, or just the API URL and team ID for self-hosted.

faq.question: What is the difference between supervised and autopilot modes?
faq.answer: Supervised mode monitors in-flight work and pushes it forward, but requires user approval before planning new work. Autopilot mode runs fully autonomously — planning, spawning workers, reviewing, and looping.

faq.question: Do workers need their own API keys?
faq.answer: Yes. Workers need an Anthropic API key to run, which you set on the team via PATCH /teams/:id. The key is stored encrypted and used only to spawn workers.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without a self-hosted instance?
faq.answer: Yes. The default SaaS path at https://agentix.cloud requires only registration and an API key. No infrastructure setup is needed.

faq.question: What task statuses are available?
faq.answer: Tasks progress through: backlog → ready → in_progress → review → done or failed. You can filter and update tasks via the API at any stage.
